anti_claim: This standard governs local observatory legibility only. It does not authorize release, hosting, publication, provider calls, source mutation, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Observatory surfaces must state that browser legibility is not release authority.

anti_claims: The observatory bridge is not a hosted public site.
The observatory bridge does not import private macro source bodies.
The observatory bridge does not turn receipts into proof of whole-system correctness.

omission_receipt.reason: The observatory carries public-root refs, local project state refs, command endpoints, and redacted receipt handles.

omission_receipt_contract.rule: Observatory receipts must confirm private paths and private bodies are absent.

receipt_expectations: receipt must cite causal-chain sections, spine/intake/reveal endpoint visibility, projection status counts, open actionable intake cells, private-state scan, and anti-claim

source_coupling_contract.rule: Observatory endpoint refs must stay localhost-relative, and evidence refs must stay public-root relative.

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A raw JSON browser dump or marketing page that hides the causal chain.
failure_modes: browser entry requires private Atlas knowledge
browser root skips the first-screen card and opens as a lens inventory
observatory hides spine/intake/reveal behind raw JSON
observatory claims hosted-public or release readiness
observatory omits projection status counts or evidence refs
standard_payload.ontology: A local browser read model over project substrate, runtime intake status, reveal board, and evidence refs.
standard_payload.teleology: Make Microcosm legible to a cold technical reader through a browser in under ten minutes.

validation_rules: require causal-chain HTML before raw JSON
require the browser root to declare reuse of the first-screen composition card
require /project/first-screen and /project/observatory-card to be visible browser drilldowns
require first-screen landing proof to preserve hello command, shared behavior command, reader route ids, evidence-class legend, and authority ceiling handles
require spine, intake, reveal, and evidence endpoint visibility
require projection status counts and zero open actionable cells when the intake protocol reports closure
reject release, hosting, publication, provider, private-equivalence, and source-mutation overclaims
require private-state scan and redacted receipts
